title: FanOps CI Control Registry
description: Validation schema for .github/ci-control-registry.yml — the machine-readable bridge between ADR policy, workflow implementation, and live GitHub branch protection. Proposed by ADR-0100. This schema is validated deterministically by the Phase-C reconciliation checks (DC-1..DC-6); it is not executed by any workflow until those land.

status.description: Lifecycle of the registry document itself. 'proposed' until ADR-0100 is accepted.

generated_view.description: Path to the human-readable ownership table generated FROM this file. That view must never be hand-maintained (it would become a second source of truth that rots).

intended_required_contexts.description: Plane 1 — the merge policy the repo INTENDS. Reconciled against live GitHub required contexts by DC-3. Every string here must equal a control's branch_protection_context whose classification is 'required'.

rollout.description: Rollout state so DC-3 does not self-deadlock while the intended required set is promoted one context at a time in Phase E.

current_required_contexts.description: What SHOULD be live in branch protection RIGHT NOW (== live GitHub). DC-3 requires live == this; updated in the SAME PR as each Phase-E mutation.

duplicate_groups.description: Registered intentional redundancy. A control may share an invariant with another ONLY via a named group here, which must state the DISTINCT boundary each member protects (ADR-0100). Silent duplication is a validator failure.

distinct_boundaries.description: member-id -> the boundary THIS member uniquely protects

remediation.description: slice id if the duplication is transitional / being reduced

id.description: STABLE identity — never a mutable job display name. Renaming a job MUST NOT change this id.

name.description: Human name. For a workflow-backed control this equals the job's `name:` string; it is descriptive, not the identity.

invariant.description: The ONE property this control protects. If two controls share an invariant they must be in a duplicate_group.

owner.description: The single accountable governance owner (a lane or subsystem, e.g. 'ci-lane', 'arch-engine').

workflow.description: Path to the workflow file, when workflow-backed. Absent for local/scheduled-external controls.

job.description: Workflow job id (stable within the file).

parent.description: Parent control id when this is a sub-gate of a composite job.

step.description: The step `name:` or logical step this control is.

command.description: The exact command/entry point, when one exists.

classification.description: required = in live branch protection; advisory = runs on PR but does not block; scheduled = cron/dispatch only; local = pre-commit/dev hook only.

deletion_consequence.description: What protection disappears if this control is removed. The deletion test (ADR-0100 / rule R6).

duplicate_group.description: key into top-level duplicate_groups, or null

branch_protection_context.description: The exact required-context string in branch protection. MUST be present iff classification == 'required'.

failure_evidence.description: The test / negative control / proof that this control CAN fail (so it is not decorative). For blocking validators this is a named negative control.

evidence_status.description: Provenance of the line/anchor evidence, per INV-20 (anchors rot; symbols do not).

allOf.$comment: A TOP-LEVEL required control (a real GitHub check, not a sub-gate) MUST declare its branch-protection context — the anti-silent-detach mechanism (ADR-0101). Sub-gates carry `parent` and inherit blocking through it, so they are exempt.
A workflow-backed control names its workflow and job.
